title: Thinking Like Clinicians: Ranking-based Multi-instance Learning for PPG-based Hemodynamic Fluctuation Detection
authors: Daomiao Wang, Q. Hu, X. Xing, H. Wu, J. Liu, X. Fu, C. Yang
abstract: Proposed IHemoMIL, combining MIL with saliency-priority ranking-based aggregation to jointly optimize accuracy and intrinsic interpretability under weak supervision. Developed a causally-driven PPG simulation method with timestep-level annotations. Outperformed mainstream temporal models across nine datasets covering anomaly detection, BP fluctuation recognition, and human activity recognition.

title: Gaze and Go: Harnessing Visual Attention Valence in Upper-Limb Robotic Rehabilitation with Tailored Gamification and Eye Tracking for Neuroplasticity
authors: Daomiao Wang*, P. He*, Y. Wang, Z. Jian, Z. Song, Q. Hu, F. Fang, C. Yang, D. Wang, et al. (*co-first author)
venue: IEEE International Conference on Robotics and Automation
abstract: Integrated eye tracking with gaze-triggered mechanisms into an upper-limb rehabilitation robot, combined with serious game design. System fused admittance control, adaptive trajectory planning, and multisensory feedback. A 10-participant study showed significant gains in gaze concentration (↑25.92%) and tracking accuracy (↑27.27%); fNIRS confirmed enhanced motor and visual cortical activation.

title: PM²ECGCN: Parallelized Spatial-Temporal Structures of Multi-Lead ECG with Graph Convolution Network for Multi-Center Cardiac Disease Diagnosis
authors: Daomiao Wang, Q. Hu, C. Cao, X. Feng, H. Wu, S. Zhu, H. Wang, C. Yang
abstract: Proposed PM²ECGCN for GNN-based multi-center multi-lead ECG diagnosis. Modeled spatio-temporal dependencies via parallelized static and dynamic cardiac graphs, with graph attention and fast spectral pooling. Introduced feature-sharing co-training for privacy preservation and domain adaptation for data heterogeneity.

title: Diffusion Model with Self-Supervised Training for Subject-Aware Photoplethysmography Recovery
authors: Daomiao Wang, Q. Hu, X. Feng, C. Yang
venue: IEEE Biomedical Circuits and Systems Conference
abstract: Proposed SDPR, a DDPM integrating subject-aware priors with signal dynamics to restore clinical usability of degraded ECG and PPG signals.

title: Biometric Recognition Based on Scalable End-to-End Convolutional Neural Network Using Photoplethysmography: A Comparative Study
abstract: A comparative study on PPG-based biometric recognition using scalable end-to-end convolutional neural networks.

title: Label-free Anomaly Detection in Cardiovascular Signals with Persistence-informed Multi-instance Learning
venue: IEEE Journal of Biomedical and Health Informatics
abstract: Proposed PISMIL, fusing persistent homology with MIL for label-free cardiovascular anomaly detection. Topological invariants from quasi-periodic signal phase spaces generate unsupervised priors guiding attention MIL for weakly supervised fine-grained localization. Integrated an LLM semantic engine to support 'verify–confirm' clinical workflow.

title: Sense, Adapt, and Coach: Toward Closed-Loop Remote Cardiac Exercise with Proactive Regulation
abstract: Proposed an agent-driven closed-loop sensing–decision–interaction training system for remote cardiac rehabilitation. Developed a custom ear-clip PPG device for robust heart rate monitoring; applied PPO with GAE for temporal credit assignment under cardiac response latency.
